ud8.2:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
ud8.2:1.3:
And on that occasion the Blessed One was instructing, urging, rousing,
& encouraging the monks with Dhamma-talk concerned with unbinding.
ud8.2:1.4: The monks–receptive, attentive, focusing their entire awareness, lending ear–listened to the Dhamma.
ud8.2:2.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud8.2:3.1: It’s hard to see the unaffected,
ud8.2:3.2: for the truth is not easily seen.
ud8.2:3.3: Craving is pierced in one who knows;
ud8.2:3.4: for one who sees, there is nothing.
